content_review: NOT_APPLICABLE — no governed SSOT content files touched. All changes are within the upload-review seam: frontend components, display helpers, type definitions. Backend upload.py, llm_parser.py, deterministic_parser.py read and verified but no backend SSOT or scoring-policy files in scope.
behaviour_review: COMPLETE — MIXED correct. This sprint changes how parsed biomarker state is displayed and edited before analysis submission. It affects: (1) how inequality-style reference values are rendered in the review table; (2) what the user can enter in the edit dialog value field; (3) how raw reference text is presented as context. These changes affect the quality of user-corrected input entering analysis, even though analytical scoring logic is not touched. Determinism preserved — same reviewed input produces same analysis payload via unchanged referenceRangeToPayload() (uploadReferenceRange.ts:100-113). No hidden repair logic permitted. No second authority source introduced. Three determinism risks surfaced: (1) formatReferenceRangeDisplay() currently coerces all one-sided bounds to ≤/≥ — if sprint preserves original < vs > from referenceText, display must not affect payload; (2) EditDialog value field change from type=number to allow string — validation must not silently accept bad data; (3) referenceText read-only status change must not break existing editable flow for users who want to correct it.

changes.check: 2A — backend/app/routes/upload.py exists; parser output fields confirmed
changes.citation: backend/app/routes/upload.py:128-145 — enriches biomarkers with SSOT metadata; does not modify range fields. Output dict keys confirmed via llm_parser.py:333-342: { id, name, value, unit, referenceRange (raw string), confidence, ref_low (float|None), ref_high (float|None), healthStatus }. deterministic_parser.py:68-79 emits same shape with referenceRange: '' and ref_low/ref_high: null.

changes.check: 2A — llm_parser.py _parse_reference_range() confirmed handles inequality symbols
changes.citation: backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py:161-188 — patterns include r'<\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)' (line 173) and r'>\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)' (line 174) and r'≤\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)' (line 175) and r'≥\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)' (line 176). For < and ≤ patterns: returns (None, float(group1)) at line 182. For > and ≥ patterns: returns (float(group1), None) at line 184. Backend correctly emits one-sided ref_low/ref_high from inequality reference strings.

changes.check: 2A — llm_parser.py value field type confirmed float-only — inequality values in value field would be silently dropped
changes.citation: backend/services/parsing/llm_parser.py:25 — ParsedBiomarker.value: float = Field(...). If Gemini returns an inequality as the VALUE (e.g. '<0.05'), pydantic coercion fails; biomarker is silently skipped at line 244 (except block continues). Sprint scope does not require fixing this — Gemini typically returns numeric values; inequality context lives in the reference field. Cursor must not change this behaviour without explicit re-authorisation.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx exists; parse row mapping confirmed correct
changes.citation: frontend/app/(app)/upload/page.tsx:218-229 — uses buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow(row) from uploadReferenceRange.ts (line 220). Value passed through as number|string (line 224). referenceRange and referenceText from helper (line 221). This mapping is correct and complete — no defect in page.tsx transformation.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts exists; one-sided bound capture confirmed correct
changes.citation: frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts:41-55 — buildReferenceRangeFromParserRow(): ref_low/ref_high independently resolved (lines 41-43), one-sided referenceRange emitted when hasLow OR hasHigh (line 46). Lines 128-141: formatReferenceRangeDisplay() formats one-sided as '≥ X' (line 136) or '≤ X' (line 137). Lib is correct. Real defect: display coerces original '< 200' to '≤ 200' — strict inequality distinction lost when numeric bounds exist.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts: inequality coercion defect confirmed
changes.citation: frontend/app/lib/uploadReferenceRange.ts:136-137 — formatReferenceRangeDisplay() always renders max-only bound as '≤ X' and min-only bound as '≥ X', regardless of whether the original reference text used strict < or >. When referenceText carries '< 200', the displayed format '≤ 200' misrepresents the lab's original strict bound. Fix must use referenceText to recover original inequality direction where present, without affecting referenceRangeToPayload() (lines 100-113) which is payload-only and must not be changed.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx exists; value field blocks inequality strings
changes.citation: frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx:249 — value input uses type='number'. Browser strips non-numeric characters including inequality symbols. Line 122: validation calls parseFloat(formData.value) and checks isNaN(numValue) — rejects any non-numeric value. If a user attempts to enter a value like '<0.05', the input field prevents this entirely. Sprint must make value field accept strings (matching ParsedBiomarker.value: number | string from parsed.ts:10).

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx: referenceText presented as editable input, not read-only assistive
changes.citation: frontend/app/components/preview/EditDialog.tsx:341-349 — referenceText field is a standard mutable <Input> with onChange handler. There is no read-only display, no visual distinction from user-entered data, and no guidance that it represents the lab's original raw text. The user can accidentally overwrite the original parsed reference context. Sprint requirement §4 (prompt lines 136-143): raw parsed values 'must be selectable/copyable or otherwise clearly presented as a read-only assistive field'.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx exists; inequality and rich text display confirmed working
changes.citation: frontend/app/components/preview/ParsedTable.tsx:169 — formatReferenceRangeDisplay(biomarker) renders range in Range column. Lines 33-64: rangeBadge() uses rangeAttentionLevel() to show 'Range incomplete' (partial) or 'Range needed' (missing). Attention UX from BE-W1-PR1 is present and must be preserved. No regression target for ParsedTable attention UX beyond preservation.

changes.check: 2A — frontend/app/types/parsed.ts exists; ParsedBiomarker type confirmed supports string values
changes.citation: frontend/app/types/parsed.ts:10 — value: number | string. referenceRange?: { min?: number; max?: number; unit: string } at line 23 (already updated in BE-W1-PR1 — min/max are optional). referenceText?: string at line 26. Type layer already supports the required shapes. The only type gap is the value field — string is allowed by the type but EditDialog.tsx:249 blocks it at the input layer.

changes.check: 2A — No duplicate authority source for reference ranges confirmed
changes.citation: Single authority chain: lab parser (llm_parser.py) or user edit (EditDialog.tsx) → uploadReferenceRange helpers → upload/page.tsx → analysis payload → normalize.py. No SSOT range registry accessed. Consistent with STOP condition 3 and non-goal (prompt line 203): 'no new second authority source for biomarker ranges'.

changes.check: 2A — Rich reference text capture confirmed working end-to-end
changes.citation: uploadReferenceRange.ts:34-39 — captures b.reference (string) and b.referenceRange (string) into referenceText. ParsedTable.tsx:169 — displays referenceText via formatReferenceRangeDisplay() when no numeric bounds. editDialog.tsx:105 — initialises formData.referenceText from biomarker.referenceText. Capture and basic display are present. Gap: for complex markers (e.g. prolactin) with multi-line text, the single-line Input in EditDialog truncates without visual indication — prominent multi-line surfacing needed.

changes.check: 2B — Behavioural scope defined; Intelligence Core confirmed not touched
changes.citation: Prompt non-goals (lines 199-207): no scoring-policy changes, no fallback-range behaviour, no parser rewrite, no results-page/narrative/Gemini work, no questionnaire work, no second authority source. Sprint scope confirmed to: EditDialog.tsx, formatReferenceRangeDisplay in uploadReferenceRange.ts, ParsedTable.tsx (preservation only), upload/page.tsx (no change needed). No pipeline, analytics, ssot, or core/ files touched.

changes.check: 2B — Regression targets confirmed and testable
changes.citation: Prompt §Regression Targets (lines 249-284): one-sided bounds survive parse→review→edit→payload; inequality cases not silently broken in UX; rich reference text preserved where captured; raw parsed value usable in edit; edited one-sided entries persist; no regression to normal min/max editing; corrected values still flow through existing payload contract (referenceRangeToPayload() at uploadReferenceRange.ts:100-113 — unchanged). All verifiable via targeted tests and browser walk-through.

changes.check: 2B — STOP conditions reviewed; none triggered
changes.citation: STOP1: inequality handling stays in upload-review seam, no scoring change needed. STOP2: rich reference text already captured in referenceText field — no parser rewrite required. STOP3: no second authority source needed — referenceText IS the preserved lab text. STOP4: changes are bounded to EditDialog.tsx + uploadReferenceRange.ts display helper + ParsedTable.tsx (preservation) — no broad redesign. STOP5: raw value usability improvement is bounded to EditDialog only. STOP6: no fallback ranges required or introduced.

changes.check: 2B — Determinism risks surfaced
changes.citation: Risk 1: formatReferenceRangeDisplay() inequality direction fix — must only affect display, NOT affect referenceRangeToPayload() (uploadReferenceRange.ts:100-113). Payload contract unchanged. Risk 2: EditDialog value field change from type=number to text — validation must still reject clearly invalid non-numeric non-inequality inputs; must not silently accept garbage. Risk 3: referenceText display mode change (read-only vs editable) — if made read-only, the user loses ability to manually correct the text; Cursor must decide whether to make it read-only or retain editability with clearer labelling. Prompt §4 (line 140-143): 'selectable/copyable or otherwise clearly presented as a read-only assistive field' — intent is read-only but Cursor may provide selectable text element or clearly labelled read-only input.

changes.check: Stage 0 — branch pre-condition
changes.result: NOTE — BLOCKING before kernel start only
changes.citation: git branch --show-current returns 'main'. Prompt requires branch: feature/parse-inequalities-and-rich-reference-text. Cursor must checkout or create this branch before running run_work_package.py start. Not a hardening blocker — branch does not need to exist at hardening time.
